exemplar.title: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Focused engagement with the prescribed title, clear definitions, and sustained relevance (Criterion A).
Relevant, concrete examples from both natural sciences and the arts that illustrate key arguments (Criterion B).
Awareness of alternative perspectives and ethical considerations, broadening the discussion beyond a single viewpoint (Criterion C).
Multidisciplinary reference list showing breadth, and a conclusion that balances implications for both areas of knowledge (Criterion D).
weaknesses: Lines of reasoning and knowledge questions are sketched rather than fully developed, limiting depth of analysis (Criterion B).
Evaluation of counter-claims and other perspectives is asserted rather than critically weighed, reducing overall analytical rigor (Criterion C).
Inconsistent bibliographic formatting and occasional structural lapses (abrupt transitions, incomplete sentences) undermine clarity and academic rigor (Criterion D).
Limited systematic exploration of how resource constraints shape methodological assumptions in each area of knowledge (Criterion B).

annotations.comment: Reference entries show inconsistent formatting (e.g., varying styles for access dates and URLs), which undermines academic rigor.
The call to balance ambition with sustainability provides a clear, actionable resolution that aligns with TOK considerations of implications.
The reference list shows commendable breadth, drawing on both scientific and cultural sources to support a multidisciplinary discussion.
The description of government initiatives (scholarships, fellowships) provides concrete support for how resources are mobilised to sustain traditional arts.
The rhetorical question in the first part of the conclusion invites reflection but replaces analytic synthesis with speculation, diluting argumentative impact.
The research question is posed rhetorically but not developed into a clear TOK knowledge question, limiting the analytical depth of subsequent discussion.
The student raises the ethical dimensionâwhether funds might be better allocated to social needsâeffectively broadening the analysis beyond purely technical concerns.
The contrast drawn between objectivity in the natural sciences and embodied transmission in the arts offers a clear and insightful AOK comparison.
Alternative viewpoints are noted but not sufficiently weighed; the student should engage more critically with counter-arguments to deepen evaluation.
The term âexclusionary pursuitâ is introduced without prior discussion or definition, making this key claim feel unsupported.
The introduction remains largely descriptive; the student should begin to hint at the methodology or criteria by which examples will be evaluated to strengthen analytical rigour.
The detailed example of the Hubble Deep Field images is appropriate and vividly illustrates how scientific curiosity pushes boundaries despite resource constraints.
The student provides clear definitions of ârelentless driveâ and âfinite resources,â establishing a solid conceptual framework that supports focused exploration of the prescribed title.
The introduction remains firmly tied to the title by immediately linking definitions to consequences in the natural sciences and the arts, demonstrating focused engagement.
The transition into the first body paragraph is abrupt and the sentence is incomplete, suggesting a need for smoother structural linking and proofreading.
The discussion of maintenance costs is descriptive but lacks critical evaluation of how such financial constraints shape research priorities in astronomy.
The parenthetical note about incorporating the scientific method remains unaddressed, indicating a gap in the studentâs plan to analyse methodology explicitly.
Evaluation of digital archives vs. live teaching remains descriptive; the student should assess trade-offs in terms of authenticity, access, and cost.
The reference to reduced practice during COVID is vague; the student should provide specific data or case studies to substantiate claims of scarcity.
The example of the Intangible Cultural Heritage programme is well chosen, illustrating institutional responses to resource pressures in the arts.
The personal reflection on learning Kathak online enhances engagement and effectively highlights the limitations of digital transmission.
The claim that digital archives âcannot replaceâ live experience is asserted without considering potential hybrid models, limiting balanced analysis.
The conclusion effectively balances implications for both natural sciences and the arts, reinforcing the essayâs coherent engagement with the title.
The bibliography relies heavily on web sources and news outlets; inclusion of peer-reviewed or authoritative academic works would strengthen credibility.
Some entries lack full author attribution or clear publication dates, making it difficult to verify sources and track academic provenance.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
